workflow.name: Onboard employees from Google Forms with Slack, Jira, and GitHub access
workflow.description:
## How it works
1. New Google Form response triggers the workflow
2. Checks if employee was already onboarded (prevents duplicates)
3. Adds user to department-specific Slack channel
4. If in Software department, grants GitHub repo access
5. Invites user to Jira and creates an onboarding task
6. Updates Google Sheet status to "Completed"

## Set up steps
Estimated setup time: 10-15 minutes

1. Connect Google Sheets (2 min) - Update sheet ID in trigger and update nodes
2. Configure Slack (3 min) - Add channel IDs and admin user ID to Code node config
3. Set up Jira (3 min) - Add project keys and component IDs to Code node config
4. Configure GitHub (2 min) - Add org name and repo names to Code node config

Detailed setup instructions are included in the sticky notes within the workflow.

parameters.content:
## Overview
This workflow automates employee onboarding using data submitted through a Google Form. Based on the employee's department and role, it provisions access to Slack, Jira, and GitHub, and tracks onboarding status.

## Who's it for
- HR teams managing employee onboarding
- IT administrators handling access provisioning
- Companies using Google Forms for new hire intake
- Teams using Slack, Jira, and GitHub for collaboration

## How it works
1. A new response is submitted via Google Forms
2. The workflow checks if the employee has already been processed
3. If already completed, the admin is notified
4. If not:
   - User is added to the appropriate Slack channel
   - If in the Software department, GitHub access is granted
   - User is invited to Jira
   - A Jira onboarding task is created
5. The Google Sheet is updated to mark onboarding as completed

## Setup Steps
1. **Google Sheets**: Connect your account and update `YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID_HERE` in both the trigger and update nodes
2. **Slack**: Connect your account and update:
   - Department channel IDs in the Code node config
   - Admin user ID in "Notify admin" node
3. **Jira**: Connect your account and update:
   - Project keys in the Code node config
   - Component IDs in the Code node config
   - Your site URL in the "Invite user to Jira" node
4. **GitHub**: Connect your account and update:
   - Organization name in the HTTP node URL
   - Repository names in the Code node config

## Requirements
- Google Sheets with form responses
- Slack workspace with appropriate channels
- Jira Software Cloud instance
- GitHub organization with repositories

## Customization
- Modify department mappings in the Code node to match your org structure
- Update Slack channel IDs for each department
- Adjust GitHub repository names for each team
- Configure Jira component IDs for different roles

### Data processing and validation
Processes form input and checks if onboarding was already completed.

### User provisioning and onboarding
Creates accounts, assigns access, and sets up onboarding tasks.

parameters.text: =Admin notice: User {{ $('Map department and team configuration').item.json['Full Name'] }} ({{ $('Map department and team configuration').item.json.Email }}) was already processed (Status = Completed)

nodes.name: Add user to department Slack channel

parameters.url: =https://api.github.com/repos/YOUR_ORG_NAME/{{ $('Map department and team configuration').item.json.targetRepo }}/collaborators/{{ $('Map department and team configuration').item.json['GitHub Username'] }}

additionalFields.description: =Please complete onboarding for new hire in department: {{ $('Map department and team configuration').item.json.Department }}

nodes.name: Update Google Sheet status to completed

main.node: Update Google Sheet status to completed
Add user to department Slack channel

user.name: Natnail Getachew
user.bio: I’m a developer and tech enthusiast currently exploring the power of n8n for workflow automation. I enjoy building web and mobile applications, experimenting with new tools, and connecting APIs to create smart solutions for daily tasks. Still learning the ropes of n8n, I’m excited to automate social media publishing, integrate AI, and streamline processes with low-code tools. Looking forward to collaborating, sharing, and discovering creative ways to boost productivity!

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
